text.title: Token Budget Breakdown
text.summary: The fixed and variable components of the 100k token budget per task, three worked estimation examples, and complexity level thresholds.

section.title: Budget Components

rule.statement: Every token budget estimate must itemize all components: agent prompt (~8k), project context (~12k), task file (~3k), design docs (relevant AD-X sections), codebase reading (list every file with size estimate), buffer (~15k). Sum must be under 100k.
rule.rationale: Unitemized estimates are consistently optimistic by 30-50%. Each component class has different variability. Itemization makes the overrun source diagnosable.

section.p: Fixed overhead per task: agent prompt 8k + project context 12k + task file 3k + buffer 15k = 38k tokens minimum.
Variable cost: codebase reading = sum of tokens in all files the agent will read. One token ≈ 4 bytes (use wc -c as proxy when ttok unavailable).

rule.statement: Include generated files (ORM clients, GraphQL schemas, migration snapshots) explicitly in the variable cost. They are 5-10x larger than hand-written equivalents.
rule.rationale: Agents consistently omit generated file sizes from estimates. A Prisma client or full OpenAPI schema can consume 20-40k tokens alone.

list.item: Simple: total context under 30k. Single file change, clear pattern, minimal dependencies. Example: database migration, utility function.
Normal: 30-60k tokens. Multiple files, some integration, standard patterns. Example: API endpoint, service class.
Complex: 60-100k tokens. Many files, deep integration, edge cases. Example: E2E test suite, complex business logic.

rule.statement: Flag tasks with complex tasks that require debugging existing code with a 30k exploration buffer on top of the standard formula. Debugging reads are non-deterministic.
rule.rationale: Standard formula assumes known files to read. Debugging passes read many files iteratively and unpredictably; the standard 15k buffer is insufficient.

example.code: Breakdown: - Agent prompt: 5k - Project context: 8k - Task file: 2k - File to modify (500 lines): ~4k - Buffer: 15k Total: ~34k tokens (simple complexity)

Breakdown: - Agent prompt: 5k - Project context: 10k - Task file: 3k - Files to modify: 15k (5 files × 3k avg) - Dependencies: 12k (4 files × 3k avg) - Buffer: 15k Total: ~60k tokens (normal complexity)

Breakdown: - Agent prompt: 8k - Project context: 15k - Task file: 5k - Files to modify: 35k (10 files × 3.5k avg) - Dependencies: 20k (6 files × 3.3k avg) - Buffer: 15k Total: ~98k tokens (complex complexity - borderline, consider splitting)
